title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout · Go Build Room
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds deployable Go feed-service release unit from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of deployable Go feed-service release unit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, secrets references, database compatibility, migration state, network policy, budgets, and dependency reachability before serving.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reproducible Image
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 7495 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to bind non-root artifacts, startup, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reproducible Image.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, sbom, signature, and vulnerability evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Reproducible Image, keep Feed Go Reproducible Image connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
